mechanism-commit-innovation.description: Commit to a concrete mechanism change and keep validation/gating secondary to the mechanism.
guardrails: Name the exact component being added/rewired and why it fixes the defect.
Use the smallest validation set that can falsify the core mechanism.
execution_logic: Generate the solution by instantiating the `atomic_blueprint`.

counterfactual-contrast.description: Add counterfactual or rare-regime data components and inject them into train/eval flow.
guardrails: Limit newly added data channels to avoid uncontrolled complexity.
Show where the counterfactual channel is injected into training and evaluation.
Keep validation focused on proving the counterfactual channel matters.
execution_logic: Generate the solution by instantiating the `atomic_blueprint`.

adaptive-constraint-hybridization.description: Hybridize constraints/penalties with the objective without making gating the main contribution.
guardrails: Keep the added constraint measurable and tied to a single objective term.
Use gating only if the constraint would otherwise violate real budget or stability limits.
execution_logic: Generate the solution by instantiating the `atomic_blueprint`.

surgical-modularity.description: Touch only one weak block via replace/rewire and prove improvement with focused ablation.
guardrails: Touch exactly one module-level block.
Include a mandatory ablation isolating the touched block.
execution_logic: Generate the solution by instantiating the `atomic_blueprint`.

data-contract-repair.description: Repair supervision/data contract first; only add modules when contract repair alone is insufficient.
guardrails: Prioritize protocol fixes before architecture changes.
Document measurable contract checks.
Add a checker only if the contract cannot be enforced with lighter-weight instrumentation.
execution_logic: Generate the solution by instantiating the `atomic_blueprint`.

multi-scale-coordinator.description: Add a coordinator for cross-scale routing/aggregation without making gating the main idea.
guardrails: Specify routing conflict resolution and added latency constraints.
Keep validation centered on whether coordination improves the core mechanism.
execution_logic: Generate the solution by instantiating the `atomic_blueprint`.

self-supervised-corrector.description: Add a self-supervised corrector to learn residuals/invariants without over-privileging gating logic.
guardrails: Define correction injection points and anti-overcorrection controls.
Keep controls lightweight and subordinate to the correction mechanism.
execution_logic: Generate the solution by instantiating the `atomic_blueprint`.

theory-transfer-injection.description: Inject a theory-backed mechanism from another domain while keeping validation/gating secondary to the transferred mechanism.
guardrails: State the transferred mechanism and where it is wired into the system.
Use gating only if transfer reliability is itself the bottleneck.
execution_logic: Generate the solution by instantiating the `atomic_blueprint`.

evaluation-contract-overhaul.description: Reshape evaluation contracts to support mechanism diagnosis while keeping evaluator changes auxiliary to the core system.
guardrails: Ensure protocol changes directly expose previously hidden failure modes.
Keep model changes secondary to evaluation contract repair.
Do not let the evaluator become the whole contribution unless the defect is purely contractual.
execution_logic: Generate the solution by instantiating the `atomic_blueprint`.
